title: TC001-post api auth login success
description: Test successful user authentication with valid credentials and company code, expecting a 200 response with access token and user details.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TIMEOUT = 30

def test_post_api_auth_login_success():
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/auth/login"
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
    }
    # Assuming valid credentials as per the test context
    # Placeholder values for username, password, and company_code
    data = {
        "username": "valid_user",
        "password": "valid_password",
        "company_code": "ae4e964a"
    }
    try:
        response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, data=data, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request failed: {e}"
    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status code 200 but got {response.status_code}"
    try:
        json_response = response.json()
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "Response is not valid JSON"

    assert "access_token" in json_response, "Response JSON missing 'access_token'"
    assert json_response.get("token_type") == "bearer", "Token type is not 'bearer'"
    user = json_response.get("user")
    assert user is not None, "'user' key missing or null in response JSON"
    assert isinstance(user, dict), "'user' is not an object/dict"
    # Optionally check some expected fields in user
    required_user_fields = ["id", "username", "roles", "company_id"]
    for field in required_user_fields:
        assert field in user, f"User JSON missing expected field '{field}'"

test_post_api_auth_login_success()

title: TC002-post api auth 2fa verify success
description: Test successful two-factor authentication verification using a valid access token and correct 2FA code, expecting a 200 response and active session.
code:
import requests

def test_post_api_auth_2fa_verify_success():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8000"
    access_token = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }
    # Assuming correct 2FA code is "123456" for testing purpose
    json_data = {"code": "123456"}

    try:
        response = requests.post(
            f"{base_url}/api/auth/2fa/verify",
            headers=headers,
            json=json_data,
            timeout=30
        )
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request failed: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 OK, got {response.status_code}"
    json_resp = response.json()
    # Validate that session is active; typically an active session might be indicated by a field
    # We check for presence of "session_active" or similar, fallback to existence of any success key
    assert "session_active" in json_resp and json_resp["session_active"] is True or \
           "message" in json_resp and isinstance(json_resp["message"], str), "Session is not active or missing confirmation"

title: TC003-get api companies authorized access
description: Test retrieval of accessible companies for an authenticated user with a valid access token, expecting a 200 response with a list of companies and active company_id.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TOKEN = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"

def test_get_api_companies_authorized_access():
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/companies"
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
        "Accept": "application/json"
    }
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request to {url} failed with exception: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status code 200, got {response.status_code}"
    try:
        data = response.json()
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "Response is not valid JSON"

    # Validate presence of companies list
    assert isinstance(data, dict), "Response JSON is not an object"
    companies = data.get("companies") or data.get("items") or data.get("data")
    # fallback if no explicit key, check if top-level is list
    if companies is None:
        if isinstance(data, list):
            companies = data
        else:
            companies = None
    assert companies is not None, "Companies list not found in response"
    assert isinstance(companies, list), "Companies data is not a list"
    assert len(companies) > 0, "Companies list is empty"

    # Validate each company item has id and name (common minimal fields)
    for company in companies:
        assert isinstance(company, dict), "Company item is not an object"
        assert "id" in company or "company_id" in company, "Company item missing 'id' or 'company_id'"
        assert "name" in company or "company_name" in company, "Company item missing 'name' or 'company_name'"

    # Validate presence of active company_id
    active_company_id = data.get("active_company_id") or data.get("activeCompanyId") or data.get("active_companyId")
    assert active_company_id is not None, "Active company_id not present in response"
    assert any(
        (company.get("id") == active_company_id or company.get("company_id") == active_company_id)
        for company in companies
    ), "Active company_id does not match any company in list"


test_get_api_companies_authorized_access()

title: TC004-post api roles create role with permissions
description: Test creation of a new role with specified permissions by an authorized user, expecting a 201 Created response with the new role id.
code:
import requests
import uuid

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TOKEN = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"
HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}
TIMEOUT = 30


def test_post_api_roles_create_role_with_permissions():
    role_name = f"test_role_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
    payload = {
        "name": role_name,
        "permissions": [
            "accounting.manage",
            "reports.view",
            "sales.create",
            "purchases.manage",
            "treasury.transfer",
            "taxes.apply"
        ]
    }
    created_role_id = None
    try:
        # Create role with permissions
        response = requests.post(
            f"{BASE_URL}/api/roles",
            headers=HEADERS,
            json=payload,
            timeout=TIMEOUT
        )
        assert response.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 Created but got {response.status_code}, response: {response.text}"
        data = response.json()
        assert "id" in data, "Response JSON missing 'id' key"
        created_role_id = data["id"]
        assert isinstance(created_role_id, (int, str)), "Role ID should be int or str"
        assert len(str(created_role_id)) > 0, "Role ID should not be empty"

        # Additional validation: Validate the role retrieved matches created role
        get_response = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/api/roles/{created_role_id}",
            headers=HEADERS,
            timeout=TIMEOUT
        )
        assert get_response.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 OK from GET role but got {get_response.status_code}"
        role_data = get_response.json()
        assert role_data.get("name") == role_name, "Role name mismatch on retrieval"
        assert set(role_data.get("permissions", [])) == set(payload["permissions"]), "Role permissions mismatch on retrieval"

        # Regression-safe read/write validation
        # Confirm permission-based access by attempting an authorized operation: list companies
        companies_resp = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/api/companies",
            headers=HEADERS,
            timeout=TIMEOUT
        )
        assert companies_resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 OK when listing companies, got {companies_resp.status_code}"
        companies_data = companies_resp.json()
        assert isinstance(companies_data, dict) or isinstance(companies_data, list), "Companies data should be list or dict"

    finally:
        # Cleanup: Delete created role if any
        if created_role_id:
            delete_resp = requests.delete(
                f"{BASE_URL}/api/roles/{created_role_id}",
                headers=HEADERS,
                timeout=TIMEOUT
            )
            # Allow 200 or 204 No Content for successful deletion
            assert delete_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Failed to delete role id {created_role_id}, status {delete_resp.status_code}"


test_post_api_roles_create_role_with_permissions()

title: TC005-get api finance accounting entries list
description: Test retrieval of journal entries and accounting records within a date range for an authenticated user, expecting a 200 response with paginated journal entries.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TOKEN = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"

def test_get_finance_accounting_entries_list():
    import datetime
    from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta

    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
        "Accept": "application/json"
    }
    # Define a date range for retrieval: last full month from today
    today = datetime.date.today()
    first_day_last_month = (today.replace(day=1) - relativedelta(months=1))
    last_day_last_month = today.replace(day=1) - relativedelta(days=1)

    params = {
        "from": first_day_last_month.isoformat(),
        "to": last_day_last_month.isoformat()
    }

    try:
        response = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/api/finance/accounting/entries",
            headers=headers,
            params=params,
            timeout=30
        )
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request to get accounting entries failed: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status_code}"
    
    try:
        data = response.json()
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "Response is not valid JSON"

    # Validate presence of pagination keys and entries list
    assert isinstance(data, dict), "Response JSON is not an object"
    # Expecting paginated result keys typically like: items or data list, total, page, per_page
    entries = data.get("items") or data.get("entries") or data.get("data")
    assert entries is not None, "Paginated journal entries list not found in response"
    assert isinstance(entries, list), "Entries data is not a list"

    # If entries exist, validate essential accounting data fields in at least one entry
    if len(entries) > 0:
        entry = entries[0]
        # Validate presence of some expected accounting entry fields
        expected_fields = {"id", "date", "journal_number", "description", "debit", "credit", "currency"}
        missing_fields = expected_fields - entry.keys()
        assert not missing_fields, f"Accounting entry missing fields: {missing_fields}"

    # Validate financial integrity hints: sums of debit and credit within response metadata if available
    total_debit = 0
    total_credit = 0
    for e in entries:
        debit = e.get("debit", 0)
        credit = e.get("credit", 0)
        # Defensive cast to float for validation
        try:
            total_debit += float(debit)
            total_credit += float(credit)
        except (ValueError, TypeError):
            assert False, f"Invalid debit or credit value in entry: debit={debit}, credit={credit}"

    # Debit and credit should be rational numbers, and accounting math integrity requires sum debit == sum credit
    # Since partial data or pagination, we can't guarantee equality but can check non-negative sums
    assert total_debit >= 0, "Total debit sum is negative"
    assert total_credit >= 0, "Total credit sum is negative"

    # Additional check: server permission and company context confirmed by expected company_id presence in token or response
    # Cannot validate permission explicitly here but rely on 200 success

test_get_finance_accounting_entries_list()

title: TC006-post api finance treasury transactions create
description: Test creation of treasury transaction records with valid data, expecting a 201 Created response with transaction id and linked journal entries.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TOKEN = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"

HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

def test_post_api_finance_treasury_transactions_create():
    try:
        # Use fixed example account IDs that should be valid in test context
        from_account_id = 123
        to_account_id = 456

        transaction_payload = {
            "type": "transfer",
            "amount": 1000.00,
            "from_account_id": from_account_id,
            "to_account_id": to_account_id,
            "date": "2026-03-01"
        }

        tx_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/finance/treasury/transactions"
        resp = requests.post(tx_url, headers=HEADERS, json=transaction_payload, timeout=30)

        assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 Created but got {resp.status_code}, response: {resp.text}"

        json_resp = resp.json()
        assert "transaction_id" in json_resp, "Response missing 'transaction_id'"
        assert "journal_entries" in json_resp and isinstance(json_resp["journal_entries"], list) and len(json_resp["journal_entries"]) > 0, \
            "Response missing linked 'journal_entries' or journal_entries list empty"

        total_debits = 0
        total_credits = 0
        for entry in json_resp["journal_entries"]:
            debit = entry.get("debit", 0)
            credit = entry.get("credit", 0)
            assert isinstance(debit, (int, float)), "Debit amount not number"
            assert isinstance(credit, (int, float)), "Credit amount not number"
            total_debits += debit
            total_credits += credit
        assert abs(total_debits - total_credits) < 0.01, f"Journal entries not balanced: debits={total_debits}, credits={total_credits}"

    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"RequestException during test: {str(e)}"
    except Exception as e:
        assert False, f"Test failure: {str(e)}"

title: TC008-post api purchases orders create
description: Test creation of purchase orders with valid supplier and line item data, expecting a 201 Created response with purchase order id.
code:
import requests
import uuid

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
TOKEN = "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..r2Ewgt6G-K7oypEt_rbzpJ8VhktrEF6qBGpAt8AdvL4"
HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {TOKEN}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

def test_post_api_purchases_orders_create():
    # Step 1: Retrieve companies to confirm active company and valid supplier/product
    try:
        resp_companies = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/api/companies", headers=HEADERS, timeout=30)
        assert resp_companies.status_code == 200, f"Failed to get companies, status {resp_companies.status_code}"
        companies_data = resp_companies.json()
        assert isinstance(companies_data, dict), "Companies response is not a dict"
        companies_list = companies_data.get("companies")
        assert isinstance(companies_list, list), "Companies key missing or not a list"

        if not companies_list:
            raise AssertionError("No companies found for user")
        company = companies_list[0]
        company_id = company.get("id")
        assert company_id is not None, "Company ID not found in companies data"

        # Step 2: Prepare purchase order payload
        purchase_order_payload = {
            "supplier_id": company_id,
            "lines": [
                {
                    "product_id": 1,  # Assuming product ID 1 exists for test
                    "qty": 10,
                    "price": 100.25
                },
                {
                    "product_id": 2,
                    "qty": 5,
                    "price": 200.75
                }
            ]
        }

        resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/api/purchases/orders",
                             headers=HEADERS,
                             json=purchase_order_payload,
                             timeout=30)
        assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 Created, got {resp.status_code}"

        data = resp.json()
        assert "id" in data, "Response missing purchase order id"
        purchase_order_id = data["id"]

        if "total" in data:
            expected_total = sum(line["qty"] * line["price"] for line in purchase_order_payload["lines"])
            returned_total = float(data["total"])
            assert abs(expected_total - returned_total) < 0.01, f"Total mismatch: expected {expected_total} got {returned_total}"
    finally:
        try:
            if 'purchase_order_id' in locals():
                resp_del = requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/api/purchases/orders/{purchase_order_id}", headers=HEADERS, timeout=30)
                assert resp_del.status_code in [200, 204, 404], f"Failed to delete purchase order {purchase_order_id}"
        except Exception:
            pass

test_post_api_purchases_orders_create()

title: TC009-get root serve frontend application shell
description: Test serving the ERP frontend application shell without authentication, expecting a 200 response with HTML content for the single-page application.
code:
import requests

def test_TC009_get_root_serve_frontend_application_shell():
    url = "http://localhost:8000/"
    headers = {
        "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8"
    }
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
        response.raise_for_status()
        # Assert status code 200
        assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status_code}"
        # Assert response content-type is HTML
        content_type = response.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
        assert "text/html" in content_type, f"Expected Content-Type to include 'text/html', got '{content_type}'"
        # Assert response content contains basic HTML elements for SPA shell
        content = response.text
        assert "<html" in content.lower(), "Response content does not contain <html> tag"
        assert "<body" in content.lower(), "Response content does not contain <body> tag"
        assert "<div" in content.lower(), "Response content does not contain <div> tag"
        # Additional heuristic: SPA app likely includes a root div with id or app-related script tags
        assert ("id=\"root\"" in content or "id='root'" in content or "app" in content.lower()), \
            "Response content does not contain SPA root container or indications"
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"HTTP request failed: {e}"

title: TC010-get api docs access swagger documentation
description: Test access to backend Swagger API documentation without authentication, expecting a 200 response with Swagger UI or JSON documentation.
code:
import requests

def test_get_api_docs_access_swagger_documentation():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8000"
    url = f"{base_url}/api/docs"
    timeout_seconds = 30

    try:
        response = requests.get(url, timeout=timeout_seconds)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request to {url} failed with exception: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status code 200 but got {response.status_code}"

    content_type = response.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    valid_content_types = [
        "text/html",
        "application/json",
        "application/vnd.oai.openapi+json",
        "application/vnd.oai.openapi",
        "application/openapi+json",
    ]
    assert any(ct in content_type for ct in valid_content_types), (
        f"Content-Type '{content_type}' is not a recognized Swagger/OpenAPI content type"
    )

    # Check presence of typical Swagger UI markers or OpenAPI JSON keys
    if "application/json" in content_type or "openapi" in content_type.lower():
        # JSON response - check for OpenAPI keys
        try:
            data = response.json()
        except Exception as e:
            assert False, f"Response content is not valid JSON: {e}"

        assert "openapi" in data or "swagger" in data, "Response JSON does not appear to be valid OpenAPI/Swagger documentation"
    else:
        # HTML response - check for Swagger UI typical strings
        text = response.text.lower()
        assert "swagger" in text or "openapi" in text or "swagger-ui" in text, (
            "Response HTML does not contain Swagger/OpenAPI UI identifiers"
        )

test_get_api_docs_access_swagger_documentation()
